title: Severe Epigastric Pain - Acute Pancreatitis
chief_complaint: Severe epigastric pain radiating to back for 12 hours, associated with nausea and vomiting. Heavy alcohol consumption over the weekend.

physical_exam.abdomen: Severe epigastric tenderness with guarding, decreased bowel sounds, Cullen sign negative, Grey Turner sign negative

hidden_data.ct_abdomen: Diffuse pancreatic enlargement with peripancreatic fluid, consistent with acute interstitial pancreatitis
hidden_data.us_abd: Gallbladder normal, no stones, pancreas obscured by bowel gas

patient_responses.alcohol: I drank heavily all weekend, maybe 15-20 drinks
patient_responses.pain: The pain is terrible, it goes straight through to my back

hints.labs: Check lipase - more specific than amylase

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! Acute pancreatitis diagnosed correctly. The lipase is diagnostic.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is acute pancreatitis. The lipase >3x normal is diagnostic, and the alcohol history is key.

ct_abdomen.comment: Confirms diagnosis and rules out complications.

feedback.attending_note: Good case. Remember: aggressive IV fluids (250-500mL/hr LR), NPO, pain control, and monitor for complications. Ranson score helps predict severity.
